title: campanula rotundifolia var. pennina
intro: Campanula rotundifolia var. pennina, also known as Harebell, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Campanula rotundifolia var. pennina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
